title: ferray-core: no byte-order / endianness handling in DType
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's dtype descriptor carries byte order ('<' little, '>' big, '=' native, '|' not applicable). This is critical for: (1) reading .npy files created on different architectures, (2) Arrow/Parquet interop, (3) network protocol buffers. ferray's DType enum has no endianness field. ferray-io must assume native byte order, which will silently produce wrong results when loading big-endian .npy files on a little-endian machine. Add a ByteOrder enum (Native, Little, Big) and plumb it through DType or as a separate parameter to from_buffer/to_buffer operations.
